jokes.text: In Japan, you must always commit suicide to avoid embarrassment.
jokes.setup: Michael sharing 'cultural facts' about international customs
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong and extreme misunderstanding of Japanese culture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - confidently delivering completely wrong information with authority. The extreme nature of the 'fact' makes it absurd.

jokes.text: In Italy, you must always wash your hands after going to the bathroom. This is considered to be polite.
jokes.setup: Continuing the pattern of 'cultural facts'
jokes.punchline: This one is actually just basic hygiene presented as exotic cultural knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good contrast joke - sets up expectation of more extreme cultural misunderstandings, then delivers something mundane as if it's exotic.

jokes.text: To Canada. Where is it? Canada.
jokes.setup: Michael's grand international business trip destination
jokes.punchline: It's just Canada, the least exotic 'international' destination possible
jokes.explanation: Classic deflation of Michael's grandiose expectations. Jim's flat delivery of 'Canada' undercuts all the international mystique.

jokes.text: the women, the pancakes, the man of mystery
jokes.setup: Michael describing what intrigues him about international culture
jokes.punchline: Pancakes as one of three mysterious international attractions
jokes.explanation: Good rule of three with pancakes as the unexpected mundane item among romantic international elements.

jokes.text: I am ashamed at your naked face. I must cover it with my jacket. You are now sexy in your culture.
jokes.setup: Michael asking Meredith to pretend to be from Abu Dhabi for practice
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate and wrong understanding of Middle Eastern culture
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe Michael - inappropriate cultural stereotyping delivered with complete confidence. The awkwardness is the comedy.

jokes.text: T-minus... 6.5 days.
jokes.setup: Jim doing a talking head about waiting for Pam's return
jokes.punchline: Using NASA countdown language for a girlfriend coming back from art school
jokes.explanation: Nice understated Jim moment - the formality of the countdown for something personal and emotional.

jokes.text: everyone here has just been so excited for me and involved and intrusive and weird
jokes.setup: Jim talking about coworkers' interest in his relationship
jokes.punchline: The escalation from 'excited' to 'intrusive and weird'
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list that captures the office's boundary issues with personal relationships.

jokes.text: I'm going to buy a sweater. Michael, that's for your food. Well, I'll just... I'll use different money for that.
jokes.setup: Michael planning to spend his meal per diem on souvenirs
jokes.punchline: His solution is to just use 'different money' as if money is categorized
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael financial logic - treating money like it has built-in categories. Very character-specific stupidity.

jokes.text: it's pretty tough to find somebody who wants to go up to Winnipeg mid-November
jokes.setup: Wallace explaining why Michael got this business trip
jokes.punchline: Revealing that this 'perk' is actually an undesirable assignment
jokes.explanation: Nice understated reveal that undercuts Michael's excitement about his 'international' assignment.

jokes.text: business-class air, like a five-star hotel in the sky... Actually better than a five-star hotel 'cause you get a big, cushy seat and you sit in a row of people
jokes.setup: Michael describing the luxury of business class
jokes.punchline: His description of why it's better actually makes it sound worse (sitting in a row with people)
jokes.explanation: Michael's backwards logic - he thinks sitting in a row with strangers is a selling point for luxury travel.

jokes.text: Two are empty. For souvenirs.
jokes.setup: Someone asking why Michael needs three suitcases
jokes.punchline: Two empty suitcases for a short business trip to Canada
jokes.explanation: Michael's over-preparation and expectation that Canada will have amazing souvenirs worth two suitcases.

jokes.text: You wanna wear that puppy right up on the breastbone like a bra. No, I don't wanna wear a bra.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to help Michael with his money belt placement
jokes.punchline: Michael's literal interpretation and rejection of wearing it 'like a bra'
jokes.explanation: Michael's childish reaction to practical travel advice, turning safety gear into gender anxiety.

jokes.text: I'm just bidding a bon voyage à la mon petite fiancée. Translation, goodbye my petite fiancée.
jokes.setup: Andy showing off his French skills
jokes.punchline: His 'translation' is just repeating the same words in English
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy pretentiousness - providing a 'translation' that isn't actually translating anything.

jokes.text: Meaning I will try to get other dudes laid. Yeah, baby. That's what I'm talking about.
jokes.setup: Michael asking what Andy means by 'I will try'
jokes.punchline: Andy's inappropriate wingman enthusiasm and Michael's approval
jokes.explanation: Typical Andy/Michael inappropriate bonding over juvenile behavior.

jokes.text: It's just a van. It's not just a van. Look, I know my way around a van. That is just a van.
jokes.setup: Michael calling their shuttle a 'town car'
jokes.punchline: Oscar's repeated insistence that it's just a van, vs Michael's denial
jokes.explanation: Michael's refusal to accept reality vs Oscar's practical assessment. The repetition builds the absurdity.

jokes.text: Michael G. Scott rolling like a pimp.
jokes.setup: Michael documenting his business class experience
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate gangster language for a corporate business trip
jokes.explanation: Michael's attempt to sound cool and street-smart while on a corporate trip to Canada.

jokes.text: Yes, this is Beth. This is my personal valet/flight attendant, and she will be helping me this morning. We need to keep the aisle clear. Yes. Get back. Get back.
jokes.setup: Michael introducing the flight attendant as his personal valet
jokes.punchline: The attendant immediately shutting down his fantasy and making him move
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Michael's grandiose self-image by reality. The immediate professional shutdown is brutal.

jokes.text: Andy brought one, too. Harry and the Hendersons.
jokes.setup: Michael bragging about his personal DVD player and 20 movies
jokes.punchline: Andy brought the same thing, but only has one terrible old movie
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Michael's impressive tech setup and Andy's single, dated movie choice.

jokes.text: Could you have picked something stinkier to bring on a plane? My God, Oscar, really. Do you have a bag of baby poop in there, too, to share with everybody?
jokes.setup: Oscar bringing egg salad sandwiches on the plane
jokes.punchline: Michael's over-the-top disgust and escalation to baby poop
jokes.explanation: Michael being overly dramatic about a minor inconvenience, escalating to gross-out humor.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry, there are no meals on flights less than two hours.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to order food like he's in first class
jokes.punchline: Reality check that this is just a short domestic flight
jokes.explanation: Another reality check deflating Michael's luxury travel fantasy.

jokes.text: I think I'm going to use my complimentary blindfold. I will don it and... Oh. Look at that, I can't see because I am in... God!
jokes.setup: Michael putting on his sleep mask like it's luxury spa equipment
jokes.punchline: He immediately walks into something because he can't see
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy from Michael's attempt to be sophisticated resulting in immediate practical consequences.

jokes.text: Is that supposed to impress me?
jokes.setup: Jim showing Karen his pushup area in the annex
jokes.punchline: Karen's unimpressed reaction to Jim's territorial display
jokes.explanation: Karen cutting through Jim's attempt to mark territory with practical dismissal.

jokes.text: Follow moi, bro-sieur.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to sound sophisticated at the hotel
jokes.punchline: Mixing French 'monsieur' with bro-speak
jokes.explanation: Good Michael portmanteau combining his attempts at sophistication with his natural dudebro tendencies.

jokes.text: A concierge is the Winnipeg equivalent of a geisha. This is a woman who has been trained in the fine art of fanciness and pleasure, and when you meet one, it is intoxicating.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining what a concierge is
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong comparison to geishas and describing hotel staff as trained in 'pleasure'
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael cultural misunderstanding - turning a hotel employee into an exotic fantasy figure. Very character-specific delusion.

jokes.text: I was. And then they switched to Acrobat just as I was learning Quark. I hate computers.
jokes.setup: Pam explaining why she's failing at graphic design school
jokes.punchline: The constant software changes making her feel behind and frustrated
jokes.explanation: Relatable frustration with technology changing faster than you can learn it. Very specific to Pam's character arc.

jokes.text: Good to see you again.
jokes.setup: Michael greeting the concierge at the bar
jokes.punchline: Acting like they have a relationship when they just met at the hotel desk
jokes.explanation: Michael creating fake intimacy with someone he just met, typical boundary issues.

jokes.text: You look, how you say, radiant tonight. And it is, how you say, a beautiful night. Michael, why? She's foreign. I...
jokes.setup: Michael trying to be suave with the concierge
jokes.punchline: Using 'how you say' for basic English words, Oscar's exasperation
jokes.explanation: Michael's completely unnecessary 'foreign accent' affectation and Oscar's mortification. Great cringe moment.

jokes.text: Everybody's going to end up dying some day, and I think it's better to die with some people that you like, like Oscar and Andy and Concierge Marie, than to know that there's somebody out there that you love that you're not with.
jokes.setup: Michael taking a group photo
jokes.punchline: Including the hotel employee he just met in his list of people he'd want to die with
jokes.explanation: Michael's tendency to create instant deep connections with strangers, mixed with morbid philosophy.

jokes.text: Come on, it's Dandy Dale and Foppy McGee over there.
jokes.setup: Andy identifying gay men to hit on
jokes.punchline: Creative derogatory nicknames that sound like cartoon characters
jokes.explanation: Andy's creative but inappropriate nicknames. The alliteration makes them memorable but the context is problematic.

jokes.text: Mademoiselle, beer me deux Long Island Iced Teas, s'il vous plaît.
jokes.setup: Andy ordering drinks in French
jokes.punchline: Mixing casual slang 'beer me' with formal French
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy pretension - trying to be sophisticated but mixing registers inappropriately.

jokes.text: Do you like apples? What? Do you like apples? Sorry, what? How do you like these apples?
jokes.setup: Andy trying to be smooth with his wingman routine
jokes.punchline: Using the 'Good Will Hunting' line completely out of context and confusing everyone
jokes.explanation: Andy misusing a movie reference as a pickup line. The confusion of the targets makes it funnier.

jokes.text: It's Christmas Eve and everything is closed and you need to get some dry-cleaning done. 12:00, midnight, where do you go? What do you do?
jokes.setup: Michael testing the concierge's knowledge
jokes.punchline: Completely ridiculous scenario - needing dry cleaning at midnight on Christmas Eve
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Michael question - creating an impossible scenario to test someone's expertise.

jokes.text: I see through a hard exterior to a little jelly in the middle.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining what he sees in Angela
jokes.punchline: Using donut/pastry metaphor for a person's emotional core
jokes.explanation: Sweet metaphor that's also slightly ridiculous - comparing his girlfriend to a jelly donut.

jokes.text: She has the softest skin I've ever seen, and I can't wait to have sex with her. You haven't had sex? No.
jokes.setup: Andy getting romantic about Angela
jokes.punchline: TMI moment followed by revelation they haven't had sex yet
jokes.explanation: Awkward oversharing followed by an even more awkward revelation about their relationship status.

jokes.text: What is wrong with you? Why won't you do Andy? What? That's Oscar, and he wants to know why you won't do me, and I think it's a valid question.
jokes.setup: Andy drunk dialing Angela
jokes.punchline: Blaming Oscar for the inappropriate question and calling it 'valid'
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe Andy moment - drunk dialing with incredibly inappropriate questions while blaming his coworker.

jokes.text: I want to take you to sex school. What? Who is that, monkey? Is somebody there?
jokes.setup: Andy continuing his drunk inappropriate calls
jokes.punchline: The absurd concept of 'sex school' and Angela's horror at someone else being there
jokes.explanation: 'Sex school' is an absurd concept delivered with drunk confidence, plus Angela's mortification.

jokes.text: Have you seen her painting, Jim? The building? There are shadows coming from two different directions. What? Are there two suns? Last I checked, that's not an office building in the Andromeda Galaxy.
jokes.setup: Dwight criticizing Pam's art
jokes.punchline: Overly technical and nerdy critique escalating to sci-fi references
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - overly pedantic art criticism that escalates to astronomy. Very character-specific nerdiness.

jokes.text: It's totally unrealistic. There are no lines in the parking lot.
jokes.setup: Dwight continuing his art critique
jokes.punchline: Focusing on the most mundane detail - parking lot lines
jokes.explanation: Dwight's obsession with practical details in art - parking lot striping as an artistic flaw is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Get the whole nine nards.
jokes.setup: Michael talking about hookup success
jokes.punchline: Mangling 'whole nine yards' into 'nine nards'
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael malapropism - close enough to the real phrase but completely wrong. 'Nards' makes it juvenile.

jokes.text: I thought I dreamed that. Oh, God. Oh, God! All right. Okay. This is so bad.
jokes.setup: Andy realizing he actually made the drunk call to Angela
jokes.punchline: The escalating panic as reality sets in
jokes.explanation: Good hangover revelation moment - the hope that it was a dream crushed by reality.

jokes.text: This can't happen again. This has to happen again. Darryl can't happen again.
jokes.setup: Jim and Karen hooking up
jokes.punchline: The contradictory statements and bringing up Karen's ex awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Post-hookup awkwardness with conflicting emotions and inappropriate ex references.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, he's gonna kill us. I'd like to see him try.
jokes.setup: Worrying about Darryl's reaction to the breakup text
jokes.punchline: Jim's false bravado immediately followed by fear
jokes.explanation: Standard tough guy posturing that will likely be immediately undercut by reality.

jokes.text: He says it's cool. He said it's cool! That's all he wrote? That's all he wrote.
jokes.setup: Expecting dramatic reaction from Darryl
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic casual response to being dumped
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of expected drama - the casual response is more unsettling than anger would be.

jokes.text: Didn't you two date for, like, a long time? Mmm-hmm. This is like a fairytale.
jokes.setup: Jim pointing out their long relationship
jokes.punchline: Karen's sarcastic 'fairytale' comment about a casual breakup text
jokes.explanation: Karen's dry observation about the unromantic nature of modern breakups.

jokes.text: What's first base with Angela? I get to kiss her forehead.
jokes.setup: Angela taking them back to first base as punishment
jokes.punchline: Revealing how chaste their relationship is - forehead kisses as first base
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela/Andy relationship joke - redefining baseball metaphors for their ultra-conservative courtship.

jokes.text: You up for a chest bump? No. Bro hug?
jokes.setup: Michael trying to bond with Oscar after their trip
jokes.punchline: Oscar rejecting multiple forms of masculine celebration
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Michael's enthusiasm and Oscar's reserved nature.

jokes.text: The trip sucked, David. It blew chunks. It was terrible. It was a bad trip. I did not like the trip.
jokes.setup: David asking about the successful business trip
jokes.punchline: Michael's unnecessarily emphatic and redundant complaints
jokes.explanation: Michael's childish tantrum using multiple ways to say the same thing. The repetition builds the absurdity.

jokes.text: which was basically just a van, and business class which was basically just coach
jokes.setup: Michael listing his complaints about the trip
jokes.punchline: Reality deflating his expectations of luxury
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier moments where reality didn't match Michael's expectations.

jokes.text: A boss that will not fire you even though you just tell him off right to his face over the phone, that's respect.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he stays at Dunder Mifflin
jokes.punchline: Confusing David's patience/professionalism with respect for his tantrum
jokes.explanation: Michael's completely backwards interpretation of his boss's restraint as validation of his behavior.

jokes.text: It's just designing logos and stuff, and I miss Scranton, but it is not because I missed you.
jokes.setup: Pam explaining why she's coming back from art school
jokes.punchline: Insisting it's not about Jim while clearly protesting too much
jokes.explanation: Classic 'the lady doth protest too much' moment - Pam's denial makes it obvious it IS about Jim.

jokes.text: I'm not going inside. All right. First thing in the morning, then.
jokes.setup: Jim asking Pam to make copies
jokes.punchline: Pam refusing to go back inside the office building
jokes.explanation: Pam's boundaries after just returning - she's back but not quite ready to fully re-enter the office world.

jokes.text: I realized that, for whatever reason, I just couldn't do better than Kelly.
jokes.setup: Ryan explaining getting back together with Kelly
jokes.punchline: Backhanded romantic declaration - 'couldn't do better'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan moment - turning a romantic reunion into a statement about settling for what you can get.
